name: Ar-Rahman Learning Center
mission: ORGANIZE, ARRANGE AND PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HUMANITARIAN SERVICE IN THE FIELD OF EDUCATION, HEALTHCARE, MEDICAL RESEARCH AND SOCIAL WELFARE THROUGH NET-WOR...
theoryOfChange: Ar-Rahman Institute (arqi.org) describes a '5-Step Academic System' as part of their approach to help students succeed. This system includes Concept Building, Practice Worksheets, Weekly Tests, Performance Analysis, and One-to-One Mentorship. Their approach is described as structured, concept-driven coaching that combines expert teaching, regular assessments, and personal mentorship to ensure consistent academic growth, aiming for excellence in school exams, board results, and competitive entrances.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2021 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Expenses are 2.2x revenue ($396,080 vs $181,050)
keyConcerns.detail: Spending significantly exceeding revenue is unsustainable without reserves drawdown.

keyConcerns.headline: Holds 786 months of reserves without clear endowment model
keyConcerns.detail: Organizations holding >36 months of reserves should publish deployment plans.

judge_issues.message: Expenses ($396,080) exceed revenue ($181,050) by 119% - may be drawing down reserves
Revenue diverges >80% across sources ($181,050 - $4,559,370) - different fiscal years (1 year spread)
Extremely high working capital (785.8 months) may indicate hoarding or data error

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 26/50, the charity offers a medium fit for donors interested in global education and social welfare [1]. The organization is categorized as sadaqah-eligible, providing a pathway for general charitable giving toward its mission of medical research and humanitarian service [4].

While it holds 785.8 months of working capital, the impact score of 30/50 reflects its focus on capacity building where cost-per-beneficiary data is not yet publicly available [1].

strengths_deep_dive: AR-Rahman Learning Center's financial health is characterized by rapid scaling and extreme fiscal conservatism. The organization reported <cite id="3">$4,559,370 in total assets</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year, a massive increase from previous periods. This growth is managed with a <cite id="1">84.1% program expense ratio</cite>, which indicates that administrative and fundraising costs are either covered by external sources or kept at a negligible level. Furthermore, the organization's <cite id="4">geographic reach spans ten countries</cite>, including Indonesia and Egypt, allowing it to implement its educational model across varied cultural contexts.
rich_narrative.summary: AR-Rahman Learning Center Inc is a New York-based humanitarian organization that provides <cite id="1">affordable services in education, healthcare, and social welfare</cite>. The organization operates with a significant financial footprint, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $4,559,370</cite> in the 2023 tax year. Its primary focus is on <cite id="4">capacity building through educational programs</cite>, including Quranic studies and Arabic language instruction, aimed at fostering spiritual and academic growth across diverse global regions. Despite its relatively recent founding in 2021, the charity has demonstrated an extraordinary growth trajectory, evidenced by a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 4242.3%</cite>. Financially, the organization maintains a lean operational model, dedicating a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 84.1%</cite> to its core missions according to recent filings. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, suggesting that the organization relies on low-cost acquisition channels or direct contributions to maximize the utility of every dollar donated. While the organization serves a global audience including the <cite id="4">USA, UK, Saudi Arabia, and Pakistan</cite>, it maintains a massive liquidity cushion with <cite id="1">785.8 months of working capital</cite> held in working capital, providing a unique level of long-term financial security for its educational initiatives.
